programme.title: Bring on the Karbs
programme.desc: Chefs Erica and Josh Karbelnik put their heads together to design a three-course menu, where each course must be based around either land, sea, or air! You have to “sea” it to believe it!

programme.title: Bring on the Karbs
programme.desc: Chefs Erica and Josh Karbelnik go head-to-head to see who can come up with the more tantalizing dish! The catch? Each of their plates must prominently feature both scallops and pork tenderloin!

programme.title: Flour Power Spring Specials
programme.desc: An ode to Sunshine and Showers, this delectable Flour Power spring themed special inspires with three deliciously creative desserts. Charming host Jessica McGovern shows that spring showers bring decadent treats such as toasted hazelnut Mud Puddle Cookies and adorable Flower Pot Cupcakes that are sure to rejuvenate and bring new life to your kitchen!

programme.title: Flour Power Spring Specials
programme.desc: Coffee klatch is an old-fashioned tradition of gathering with your friends for coffee and conversation. In this sweet half-hour special, charming host Jessica McGovern pays tribute to this custom with three decadent and impressive desserts made with fragrant coffee

programme.title: Flour Power Spring Specials
programme.desc: Bring the sunshine to your kitchen with Flour Power's sweet half-hour spring special Birds and Bees! Inspired by chirping birds and buzzing bees, charming host Jessica McGovern bakes adorable desserts, including sweet and salty Bird's Nest Cookies and an ovERS-the-top Honey Apricot Dessert Tower drizzled in sticky goodness.

programme.title: Flour Power Spring Specials
programme.desc: Lace up your aprons and have the darling children join in on the fun as bubbly host Jessica McGovern inspires with creative and delicious Kid's Bake Sale recipes. Irresistible and easy-to-make, Jessica guides us through four baked goods that will sell faster than it'll take to make.

programme.title: Flour Power
programme.desc: Country fair treats made right at home! Host Jessica McGovern makes tasty fair-inspired desserts like classic Funnel Cake.

programme.title: Flour Power
programme.desc: Which tasty treats tantalize your taste buds? Sweet and salty ones, of course! Host Jessica McGovern shows us some of her favourites.

programme.title: Fresh Market Dinners
programme.desc: Host Amanda Herrera transforms market fresh honey, cherries, garlic scapes and carrots into two simple chic sides and an irresistible dessert.

programme.title: Fresh Market Dinners
programme.desc: Host Amanda Herrera creates two summer fresh salads and a hearty breakfast using sprouts, potatoes and leafy lambs quarters.

programme.title: Fresh Market Dinners
programme.desc: Host Amanda Herrera shows us why fresh is always best with two creative dishes using ingredients from the farmers' market.

programme.title: Fresh Market Dinners
programme.desc: Zestful cook Amanda Herrera scoops up raspberries, peas, mint, and apricots to use in three summer recipes.

programme.title: CombiNation Plates
programme.desc: CombiNATION time! Barbados is the sun-drenched island of enchantment where Chef Craig starts this culinary adventure. Check out Bajan Spice Snapper with Curried Pumpkin for a Caribbean taste sensation! Next comes a feast for the eyes with Oxtail PasÌŒticada from Croatia, served with a truffle sauce that melts in your mouth, and also in your heart! Turns out that both of these countries love... shrimp! Check out the Croatian creation with tomatoes and plum brandy; for Barbados? It's grilled with spice and dipped in mango-chili sauce. The Big Dish is Monkfish wrapped in PrsÌŒut, a smoky Croatian dry-cured ham, served with scotch bonnet-spiked tomatoes and Barbadian Cou cou made with corn...

programme.title: CombiNation Plates
programme.desc: First up, Belgium is famous for its mussels and fries, aka 'Moule Frite'. Chef Wong has a unique take featuring Belgian beer steamed mussels salad, waffle cut fries, and potato-leek mousse. Next, Steak Madagascar is an old school fine-dining fave, this take, uses modernist 'spherification' for the sauce, and a vanilla bean rub for a steak that is decidedly not 'old school', wow! For a common ingredient, how about sweet breakfast? Belgians are famous for Liege Waffles, and Madagascar loves Mofogasy, a pancake made with rice flour and coconut. The final dish is inspired by Belgian Waterzooi, a root veggie stew, mixed with a popular Malagesy stir-fry with shellfish and pork... Chef carefully...

programme.title: CombiNation Plates
programme.desc: Tiraditos! No, it's not a joyous exclamation (even though it sounds a little like one...) it's a Peruvian dish that lands somewhere between traditional Peruvian ceviche and Japanese sashimi. Chef Craig Wong's version is made with swordfish and includes sweet potatoes pureed with spicy Aji Amarillo chilies. For his Lebanese tribute, check out Lamb Neck cured and slow-poached in olive oil for a melt-in- your-mouth result, paired with an intense, flavourful Black Baba Ghanoush made with charred eggplant and caramelized onions. For a common ingredient, it is two takes on potato: Lomo Saltado with grilled flank steak, and a cheese, herb, olive oil and lemon drenched Hasselback Batata Harra....

programme.title: CombiNation Plates
programme.desc: This CombiNation Plates culinary adventure starts in Portugal with a Scallop dish that features some of their finest ingredients, such as Port Wine, ChouricÌ§o sausage, Kale, and Figs. Next up is South Africa and the feature is an Ostrich Steak, served with native Cape Gooseberries, the bizarrely named "Monkey Gland Sauce" (don't worry, no monkeys and very few glands are involved). The common ingredient is Peri Peri peppers, featured first on a Grilled Sardine with Olive Soil for a peak Portugal experience, then on the Peri Peri Chicken dish that South Africa made internationally famous. The combination plate is always the big finale... Stand back in awe for Chef Craig's Giant Braaied...

programme.title: Fish the Dish
programme.desc: Chef and seafood lover Spencer Watts makes tastebuds dance as he creates recipes that combine sour and tart flavours with grouper and clams.

programme.desc: The rich heritage of hibachi meets modern griddle artistry. Dive into the origins of teppan-style cooking while exploring new sauces, sizzling techniques, and flavor-packed innovations that bring restaurant-style magic to the home griddle.

programme.title: CombiNation Plates
programme.desc: CombiNATION Plates is television's best travel show that never leaves the kitchen. Skilled Chef Craig Wong is your host, and prepare to be blown away by his takes on the foods of France and Vietnam. First up? A re-write of a popular Vietnamese street snack made with Clams and Sesame Rice Noodles. Next? Marrow Toast dresses up in its tux and tails for a version made with King Oyster Mushrooms and Sauce Bordelaise. The common ingredient is Baguette; check out supersonic versions of French Jambon Beurre and Vietnamese Banh Mi. The Big Dish blends delicate sole with ProvencÌ§ale flavour spiked Nuoc Cham and a crunchy Haricot Amandine inspired Vietnamese crepe.

programme.title: CombiNation Plates
programme.desc: Go-to Indian menu item Lamb Rogan Josh is one thing, but Lamb Sweetbreads Rogan Josh with Aloo Gobi Foam? That's a CombiNATION Plates thing! Craig Wong is at it again in this USA + India episode. After India, Chef moves on to the USA to answer the question, 'is everything better battered and fried?' with his Country Fried Torchon of Foie Gras. The common ingredient for these two distant nations is lobster, and really, who doesn't love it? For the US, it's Lobster with Cajun Spice Grits and Pickled Okra, and for India, check out Lobster Curry with Coconut Milk. The finale features Catfish like you've never seen it. Cooked Tandoori style and served with Collard Greens Paneer and Peach...

programme.title: Redwood Forest Rain
programme.desc: A one-hour loop of falling rain, filmed in Mount Rainier National Park.

programme.title: Waterfalls of the World 2
programme.desc: Places like Iguazu Falls in Argentina, Patagonia in Chile, Banff and Jasper in Alberta, McWay Falls and Hidden Falls in Big Sur, California, Columbia River Falls in Oregon, and more, paired with soothing ambient music by Relax Moods.

programme.title: Tumalog & Aguinid Falls: Philippines
programme.desc: Starting the journey marveling at the cascading turquoise waters of Tumalog Falls, nestled amidst the lush forest and tropical foliage, and staying captivatingly thrilled by Aguinid Falls' multi-tiered rapids and unique rock formations.

programme.title: Wharariki Beach I: New Zealand
programme.desc: Exploring the sandy shores and rocky cliffs of a majestic beach in New Zealand.

programme.title: Tropical beaches: Indonesia
programme.desc: A remote Indonesian island with breathtaking scenery of various beaches.

programme.title: A Day in Big Sur 1
programme.desc: The sights and sounds of Big Sur, Calif.

programme.title: Flying Over Maui
programme.desc: Soaring high above Maui, Hawaii's exotic coastline and lush interior.

programme.title: Wildlife Wonders of South America
programme.desc: A sunrise at Salar de Uyuni in Bolivia with flamingo and llama scenes from high altitude volcanic salt lakes; welcoming new life in the Chilean landscapes of San Pedro Atacama; the Torres del Paine National Park in Patagonia.

programme.title: Bryce Canyon Relaxation
programme.desc: Admiring the wonders of one of the world's most breathtaking natural destinations: Bryce Canyon, in Utah.

programme.title: Seas of the World
programme.desc: Seas of the World

programme.title: Waimamaku Beach: New Zealand
programme.desc: Exploring the remote, cliff-lined, cavernous coastline of Waimamaku Beach.

programme.title: Oahu Morning Waves
programme.desc: Relaxing on the shores of Oahu, Hawaii just after sunrise, where big blue waves crash in golden sunlight, perfect as a backdrop for meditation, yoga, relaxing, reading, or just as an idyllic flowing screensaver.

programme.title: Flying Over Scotland
programme.desc: Soaring over the lush green landscape and enchanting waters of Scotland in a bird's-eye view voyage.

programme.title: Rushing Rocky Stream: Hawaii
programme.desc: Along the Pipiwai Trail, take an unforgettable walk through Hawaii's immense and picture-perfect bamboo forest.

programme.title: Serene Waters: Diablo Lake, Washington
programme.desc: The serene calm of the Diablo Lake, surrounded by majestic mountains.

programme.title: Wonders of Nature
programme.desc: Immerse yourself in the magical and relaxing wonders of nature with an ambient relaxation video featuring world class music by Darshan Ambient; in it you'll be transported to over 30 different natural locations around the world.

programme.title: Blue Horizons
programme.desc: Featuring a diverse set of blue nature scenes from places like Hawaii, Mexico, Canada and California paired with a custom ambient track by Travis Revell.

programme.title: A Day in Santorini
programme.desc: A visual journey through the coastal village of Oia, found on the far tip of Santorini Island.

programme.title: Waimamaku Beach: New Zealand
programme.desc: Exploring the remote, cliff-lined, cavernous coastline of Waimamaku Beach.

programme.title: Nusa Penida: Indonesia
programme.desc: A bird's eye view of Nusa Penida.

programme.title: Pure Relaxation
programme.desc: Relaxing to the slow descent of the sun over calm waters.

programme.title: Lake Tahoe Shoreline Rocks
programme.desc: The shoreline of Lake Tahoe.

programme.title: Turtle Paradise
programme.desc: Dive into an undersea world inhabited by the sea turtles; featuring real-time video scenes captured from around the Caribbean, paired with gentle underwater sounds and calming music for relaxation.

programme.title: Mount Cook: New Zealand
programme.desc: Taking in the rugged beauty of snow-capped mountains and calm emerald waters.

programme.title: Tumpak Sewu Waterfalls: East Java
programme.desc: A close look at the edge of the tiered, plunging streams of Indonesia's Tumpak Sewu falls.

programme.title: Golden Waves Crashing at Sunset
programme.desc: The shores of the iconic Baker Beach in San Francisco with a sunset scene filmed in high definition. Views of the Marin headlands and the Golden Gate bridge.

programme.title: Tranquil Tide: Ca'te d'Azur
programme.desc: The medieval town of Menton on the French Côte d'Azur, where the sun glistens on iridescent water.

programme.title: Watu Leter & Goa Cina Beach: Indonesia
programme.desc: Exploring the remote coasts of Indonesian beaches with limestone stacks in the bay.

programme.title: Beach Sunrise: Hawaii
programme.desc: Emerge from slumber to the sight of a brilliant sunrise over the vast expanse of Hawaii's Oneloa Beach.

programme.title: A Day in Paradise
programme.desc: Filmed in 2013 by David Huting, the tiny island is hidden off of the northeastern coast of the outer Fiji Islands (near Taveuni), and boasts a perfect white sandy beach, groves of palm trees, and a majestic lagoon that shelters the island from all sides. It has more turtle visitors than humans!

programme.title: Superbloom Soaring
programme.desc: A gentle flight over the spring poppy fields of California's Antelope Valley and Lake Elsinore wilderness.

programme.title: South Australia Seas
programme.desc: Transporting viewers to the remote and gorgeous southern coasts of Australia as captured from above; gently traveling from one vibrant ocean scene to the next paired with real-life ocean sounds and ambient music.

programme.title: Cadlao Lagoon: Philippines
programme.desc: Experiencing the beauty of a turquoise lagoon flanked by impressive, vegetation-covered, mountains.

programme.title: Shark Paradise
programme.desc: Captured by filmmaker collaborator Peter Schneider, Shark Paradise features hundreds of unique real-time scenes captured from around the world, paired with gentle underwater sounds and calming music for deep relaxation by Relax Moods.

programme.title: White Beach: Philippines
programme.desc: Lazing the day away on a white sand beach in the Philippines as the sun gently sets on the horizon.

programme.title: Return to Paradise 1
programme.desc: Featuring the hidden exotic Fijian island of Nanuku, a tiny, palm-covered, sandy atoll surrounded by lagoons brimming with sea life; some aerial drone clips give perspective from above.

programme.title: Flying Over South Korea
programme.desc: The filmmaker showcases South Korea's natural wonders and urban landscapes by exploring the four of the country's most famous destinations: Jeju Island, Seoul, Geoje National Park and Busan.

programme.title: Redwood Forest Rejuvenation
programme.desc: Transporting to the magical redwood forests of Northern California and soaking up the healing energies that only pure nature can provide, with multiple healing scenes from the old-grove forest, paired with real-life stereo nature sounds.

programme.title: Redwood National Park: California
programme.desc: Wander beneath the dense canopy of the redwood forest, beneath some of the tallest trees in the world.

programme.title: Wairere Falls: New Zealand
programme.desc: The Wairere Falls of New Zealand.

programme.title: Chemin Pealey: Îles de la Madeleine
programme.desc: Admiring the tranquility of water.

programme.title: Boardwalk to the Beach: Guadeloupe
programme.desc: Strolling on the boardwalk to the turquoise waters in Le Gosier, Guadeloupe.

programme.title: Hamilton Island Palms
programme.desc: Soaking up the tropical breeze with the stunning palm and coastal vista from the famous Hamilton Island, Australia.

programme.title: Santa Ynez Valley: California
programme.desc: Take a walk in the idyllic valley and enjoy calming sights of mature trees, and endless skies.

programme.title: Ruby Beach Sunset
programme.desc: Relax for a full hour as a golden, soothing sunset slowly unfolds and turns to dusk as seen from Ruby Beach, on Washington state's Olympic Peninsula; you'll be lulled into a state of tranquil relaxation thanks to the sounds of rhythmic ocean waves gently crashing over the pebbly beach paired with the dazzling collage of four different sunset scenes shot in 4K.

programme.title: Mountains of Majesty
programme.desc: Sights and sounds of the Rocky Mountains in Banff and Jasper in Alberta, Yoho National Park in British Columbia and Glacier National Park, Mont.; featuring raging waterfalls, clear mountain lakes, snowy mountains, wildflowers and abundant wildlife.

programme.title: Underwater Wonders
programme.desc: Highlighting the abundant and varied flora and fauna that dwells underwater in several oceans around the world.

programme.title: Relaxing Nature Sceneries
programme.desc: The filmmaker provides a collection of peaceful nature scenes and tranquil vistas that will soothe body and soul.

programme.title: Tutoko River: New Zealand
programme.desc: Rushing waters through rocky rapids.

programme.title: Kaštela (U masliniku)
programme.desc: In Kaštela, a cluster of seven towns near Split, winemaking runs deep. Among olive groves and vineyards, Mladen Kuzmanić treated us to local flavors—sipa i bob, pancetta, goat cheese, and homemade sausage—perfectly paired with a glass of regional wine.

programme.title: Med Nations 03 Turkey
programme.desc: Turkey, where cultures, continents, and centuries of history all come together. Turkey has a lot of different traditions, flavours, and history that have been shaped by powerful empires and cultural crossroads that have been around for hundreds of years. You can see this from the busy streets of Istanbul to the sunny Mediterranean coast.

programme.title: Med Destinations Delos
programme.desc: Step into the myth and mystery of Delos with Med Destinations. This tiny island, rich in archaeological wonders and legendary tales, offers a unique glimpse into Greece’s ancient past amid stunning coastal vistas.

programme.title: Whats On Lights, Cinema, Mediterranean
programme.desc: The Mediterranean screens its stories this week. Malta hosts the Mediterranean Film Festival, while Italy Venice Film Festival and Med Film Festival bring glamour and regional storytelling to iconic locations. France Cannes Film Festival shines globally, Spain International Fantastic Film Festival of Catalonia celebrates genre cinema, and Morocco Amazigh Film Festival showcases North African voices. Turkey Adana Golden Boll Film Festival and Croatia Zagreb Film Festival round out a cinematic journey across the Med.

programme.title: Med Destinations Crete
programme.desc: Med Destinations takes you to Crete, the jewel of the Greek islands, where ancient history meets breathtaking landscapes. Explore charming villages, hidden beaches, and the vibrant local culture that makes Crete a must-visit Mediterranean paradise.

programme.title: Gold Cuisine
programme.desc: Step into a world of fresh flavors with a zesty tuna sandwich loaf loaded with crisp veggies and herbs, followed by a luscious banana Swiss roll layered with creamy pastry filling.

programme.title: Rick Steves Europe
programme.desc: Experiencing Madrid at its fun-loving best, we stare down a flamenco dancer, nibble pigs' ears, and ogle lavish palaces before side-tripping for some unforgettable history.

programme.title: Med Destinations Lake Bled
programme.desc: Step into the fairytale beauty of Lake Bled, Slovenia, on Med Destinations. Crystal-clear waters, a picturesque island church, and lush Alpine surroundings make it a serene Mediterranean escape.

programme.title: Elain Dutton: Agents of Change Panel
programme.desc: Building in the Mediterranean today is about more than beauty-it’s about survival and legacy. This panel, Agents of Change, spotlights the developers, architects, and planners driving resilient, sustainable, and climate-ready projects that will shape the region for generations.

programme.desc: The Mediterranean screens its stories this week. Turkey Adana Golden Boll Film Festival and Croatia Zagreb Film Festival round out a cinematic journey across the Med.

programme.title: Malini Angelica Napoli
programme.desc: Malini Angelica visits Naples, in Italy’s vibrant Campania region. She dives into the city’s beautiful chaos to uncover the heart of Neapolitan culture, sampling delicious street food, meeting friendly locals, and exploring its rich history. With its authenticity and energy, Naples stands out as one of Italy’s most captivating cities.

programme.title: Rick Steves Europe
programme.desc: Naples is Italy in the extreme. We’ll prowl backstreet fish markets and dodge speedy Vespas before indulging in pizza where it was invented. Afterward, we'll climb to the top of nearby Mount Vesuvius, then wander through the ruins of Pompeii, the Roman town it destroyed.

programme.title: Med Destinations Delos

programme.title: Vivi e lascia vivere
programme.desc: After the sudden death of her husband in a mysterious fire, Laura Ruggero returns to Naples, determined to rebuild her life while keeping a secret from her three children. Struggling with financial troubles and a strained relationship with her eldest daughter, Giada, Laura loses her job but seizes the chance to start a new business. As she reconnects with an old acquaintance, Toni, her children face their own challenges—Giada turns to nightclub dancing, Nina’s reckless behavior causes trouble, and the family’s buried secrets begin to surface.

programme.title: Med Nations 03 Turkey
programme.desc: Turkey, where cultures, continents, and centuries of history all come together. Turkey has a lot of different traditions, flavours, and history that have been shaped by powerful empires and cultural crossroads that have been around for hundreds of years. You can see this from the busy streets of Istanbul to the sunny Mediterranean coast.

programme.title: Presente
programme.desc: Daniela confesses to having a crush on Francesco, and Olivia lends herself to being her Cyrano, but, as always, she makes one trouble after another. Eros is in crisis with Martina, unsure about his feelings and his relationship, while Viola continues her investigation to discover the truth about her father's identity.

programme.title: Med Minds 03 Cleopatra
programme.desc: Cleopatra, the last Pharaoh of Egypt, captivated empires with her intelligence, charm, and enduring influence.

programme.title: Concerto - OSN Marc Albrecht E Simone Lamsma Torino
programme.desc: In this 2022 concert in Turin, Marc Albrecht conducts the Orchestra Sinfonica Nazionale della Rai, with Simone Lamsma as the violin soloist. The programme includes Shostakovich's Violin Concerto No. 1, showcasing Lamsma's technical brilliance and emotional depth, and Franck's Symphony in D minor, celebrated for its rich harmonies and cyclical structure. The performance highlights a powerful blend of passion and precision.

programme.title: Outdoor and Nature

programme.title: Imotska krajina
programme.desc: Imotski, known for its stunning Red and Blue Lakes, draws nature lovers and active travelers alike. During a local marenda break, host Jurica Grbavac introduced us to regional flavors—pivca and pura, a true taste of Imotski tradition.

programme.title: Malini Angelica Lisbon
programme.desc: Malini Angelica explores Lisbon, the vibrant capital of Portugal. Once founded by the Phoenicians and shaped by Romans, Moors, and explorers of the Golden Age, Lisbon’s layered history is reflected in its culture and charm. During her visit, the city comes alive with the Festas Populares de Lisboa — a June celebration of Saint Anthony filled with music, dancing, and barbecued sardines. Malini samples local favorites like grilled sardines, bifanas, and the famous pastel de nata, and experiences the soulful beauty of Fado music. Lisbon’s warmth, flavor, and festive spirit show why it remains one of Europe’s most beloved cities.

programme.title: Rick Steves Europe
programme.desc: We explore the dramatic castles, prehistoric cave paintings, and prized cuisine of the Dordogne River Valley, with its unforgettable blend of man-made and natural beauty.

programme.title: Property Transfer: Protection vs Efficiency
programme.desc: As property markets evolve, the laws governing ownership and transfer must evolve with them. This panel investigates how legal structures can protect buyers and investors without slowing progress, and what reforms are needed to ensure efficient, transparent, and future-ready property transfer across the Mediterranean.

programme.title: Bronzi di Riace il tesoro ritrovato
programme.desc: Calabria, a land steeped in myths, reveals its secrets through the Riace Bronzes. Restorer Cosimo Schepis shares the story of these ancient treasures, 50 years after their discovery. Actress Swamy Rotolo and her sister Grecia journey through Calabria, uncovering its hidden gems. A captivating exploration of the myths and legacy of Magna Grecia.

programme.desc: WITH VO

programme.title: Rick Steves Europe
programme.desc: Slovenia, a small nation of just two million people, is one of Europe’s most surprising delights. Nestled at the crossroads of the Germanic, Mediterranean, and Slavic worlds, it offers a charm all its own.

programme.title: Colours of Mediterranean Culture
programme.desc: From Malta to Greece, Mediterranean arts flourish. Valletta Baroque Festival, Malta International Arts Festival, and Birgu by Candlelight light up the island with music and heritage. Italy’s Cortona on the Move, La Fenice in Piazza San Marco, and Mercantia Festival showcase photography, opera, and street performance. France’s Art Paris and International Garden Festival, Spain’s Revela’t Festival and La Leyenda de Tartessos, Portugal’s Umbrella Sky Project and Walk & Talk Biennale, Turkey’s Istanbul Theatre Festival and Biennial, and Greece’s Athens & Epidaurus Festival highlight the Med’s rich artistic tapestry.

programme.title: Malini Angelica Menorca
programme.desc: Malini Angelica explores Menorca, from Mahon’s markets and history to Ciutadella’s gothic charm and northern beaches. In Es Migjorn Gran, she hikes to ancient caves, visits Binigaus beach, and also learns to make Mahon style cheese.

programme.title: Med Destinations Rimini

programme.title: Malini Angelica Sicily
programme.desc: Malini Angelica explores Sicily’s enchanting southeast, visiting Catania, Noto, Syracuse, and Ortigia. She dives into Catania’s lively fish market, samples local dishes like pasta alla norma and even horse meat burgers, and enjoys wine tasting in Noto’s vineyards. In Syracuse and Ortigia, she discovers stunning Greek ruins and seaside charm that showcase Sicily’s rich culture and history.

programme.title: Marvels of the Mediterranean
programme.desc: To recount Lebanon's history means going back to the entire Mediterranean culture's roots. The Beirut National Museum pays tribute to its country's history: from the invention of glass paste, phonetic alphabet and marine to being the biggest commercial exchanges driver in Ancient Mediterranean.

programme.title: Dates that made history
programme.desc: The fall of Constantinople marked the end of the Byzantine Empire – a power descending from none other than ancient Rome. Its symbolism has long since transcended its actual significance.

programme.title: Rick Steves Europe
programme.desc: When Rome was little more than a village, Athens was already laying the foundations of Western civilization. And today, amid its rich heritage, Greece’s vibrant capital continues to thrive.

programme.title: Concerto per Milano 2022
programme.desc: The Filarmonica della Scala and principal conductor Riccardo Chailly return to Piazza Duomo for the evocative Concerto per Milano. A landmark cultural event, this open-air performance brings world-class music to the heart of the city. Television direction by Stefania Grimaldi captures the grandeur and emotion of the evening.

programme.title: Sinj
programme.desc: Sinj blends courage, tradition, and warm hospitality—reflected even in its food. Edo Crljen welcomed us with a bold serving of tripes, while Ankica Vlastelica prepared the town’s pride: arambaši, Sinj’s iconic stuffed cabbage rolls you simply can’t leave without tasting.

programme.title: Great World Hotels
programme.desc: Rising graciously from the Arabian Gulf towards the azure sky, Atlantis, the Palm stands on the crescent of the iconic Palm Jumeirah. The exotic resort captures the world’s imagination with its mystical aura.

programme.title: Backpack Serbia
programme.desc: Heather explores Nis,one of the oldest cities in Europe.She visits a concentration camp and later paraglides to end by watching a musical performance.

programme.title: Xplore Austria
programme.desc: Elisha discovers the contemporary side of Vienna at some of its trendiest, most lively neighborhoods.

programme.title: The Savvy Nomad Phillippines
programme.desc: In the last leg of her journey, Emeline ventures into the captivating realm of Coron with its mesmerizing lagoons.

programme.title: 10 Days Scotland
programme.desc: The grand finale of Robbie’s wild homecoming unfolds amidst the rugged beauty of the Isle of Skye.

programme.title: The Food Guide
programme.desc: Traditional Hungarian fare is a hearty affair, not meant for the faint-hearted. The cuisine has imbibed influences from the days of its Ottoman occupation as well as from the other nations of the Austro-Hungarian Empire and traditional Jewish cuisine. Celebrity chef and traveller, Joe Remblance explores the nuances of this cuisine in Budapest, the capital city, through local dishes such as Schnitzel, Gundel Palacsinta, Hungarian Lángos and Tokaji, a sweet Hungarian wine.

programme.title: World Spas
programme.desc: From an underwater therapy to a massage that detoxes internal organs, Jamie Srubis tries varied spa treatments at the Chiva Som resort in Thailand.

programme.title: Backpack Serbia
programme.desc: Continue exploring Serbia with Heather as she visits the Manasija monastery, Resavska caves, ending with a joyride onboard Eight of Sargan rail line.

programme.title: Xplore Austria
programme.desc: Elisha is starstruck by the musical heritage of Salzburg, which was the setting for Mozart’s compositions and the story of the Von Trapp family.

programme.title: World Heritage
programme.desc: From the impact of maritime trade and the European Renaissance on Portugal in the 15th century to the nationwide Romanticism movement in the 19th century, the heritage sites of Portugal such as Jeronimo Monastery and Pena Palace reveal various stages of the country’s evolution.

programme.title: City Breaks
programme.desc: Valencia, the birthplace of paella, has got the bones of a metropolis and the heart of a quaint seaside town. Alex Outhwaite experiences this when she visits the strikingly futuristic City Of Arts And Sciences and then tries the local specialty, Pinxtos at a Tapas Bar and enjoys Valencia’s laidback charms at El Cabanyal Beach. She also encounters the city’s strong Catholic roots at Cathedral de Valencia, the alleged home of the Holy Grail used by Christ at the Last Supper.

programme.title: The Food Guide
programme.desc: The dining scene in Manchester has changed considerably over the last 20 years. Stuffy fine dining places have been replaced with street food and flavours from across the globe. Joe Remblance explores this cosmopolitan approach that has seeped into Manchester’s culinary scene though European dishes such as Black Pudding and Tzaziki, Asian dishes such as Chicken Curry and innovative dishes such as a fried chicken waffle pizza and deep-fried Cadbury Crème Eggs.

programme.title: World Spas
programme.desc: Jamie Srubis tries spa treatments inspired by ancient Balinese healing remedies at the Spa Village Resort in Tembok, Bali.

programme.title: Backpack Serbia
programme.desc: In her final leg of journey, Heather visits the Uvac canyon, tries locally prepared pie, ending with Kopaonik National Park and Jelovarnik waterfalls.

programme.title: Xplore Austria
programme.desc: On a trip to Innsbruck, Elisha finds out stories of a 15th century emperor, under whose reign the city had reached its zenith.

programme.title: Timeless Tamil Nadu
programme.desc: In the last leg of her journey in Tamil Nadu, Alex visits one of India’s southernmost towns, Rameshwaram. Here, she is treated to stunning views of azure waters and coral islands that stretch all the way to Sri Lanka. What she doesn't expect to find, however, are the ruins of a ghost town sitting on a beach.

programme.title: 10 Days Scotland
programme.desc: Robbie’s journey to reconnect with his Scottish roots begins in Edinburgh, the stunning capital city perched on extinct volcanoes. Navigating the twists of the Royal Mile, strutting around the majestic Edinburgh castle and learning to sip scotch like a true Scotsman, he feels a tad closer to his heritage. The real fun kicks in, however, when he stumbles onto a rather flamboyant way to unleash his inner Highlander.

programme.title: Quest
programme.desc: While the vast majority of Rajasthan’s inhabitants have always been staunch vegetarians, there has been tremendous popularity of the meat dishes in the state’s culinary culture. This came mainly from the Rajput rulers, which is why Alex meets Ajayraj Singh who comes from a royal lineage. He introduces her to the history of the valiant rulers of Mewar and some of the secret meat preparations of the kingdom

programme.title: Xp Guide
programme.desc: With a rich history dating back to over 1000 years, Oslo’s iconic monuments reveal its evolution through the ages to visitors.

programme.title: Backpack Serbia
programme.desc: Visit Belgrade, with Heather as she explores the city, its architecture and learns about its past by visiting historical monuments.

programme.title: Xplore India
programme.desc: Aradhana embarks on an adventure to find out the hidden wonders of a land steeped in spirituality and untamed wilderness: Chhattisgarh!

programme.title: Quest
programme.desc: Where many ancient Indian civilizations flourished in the most resource-filled areas, the people of Rajasthan created a powerful, complex, layered and regal culture and cuisine in the largely arid land that they called home. Alex discovers this when she meets Anjali Singh, a member of the royal family of Jaipur, who shows her how to make two prized recipes from her family’s legacy - Papad Ki Chutney and Safed Murg.

programme.title: Xplore India
programme.desc: As Aradhana’s journey in Chhattisgarh continues, she is stunned to discover the wealth of natural beauty that the state has to offer.

programme.title: 10 Days Scotland
programme.desc: Robbie’s adventures in Perth kick off with him immersing himself in the historic grandeur of the Scone Castle, where a mysterious stone tells tales of Scottish monarchs. But the real thunder of the Highlands awaits at the Braemar Games gathering, where tartan meets brawn, and Robbie learns that embracing his Scottish side involves more than just kilts and castles.

programme.title: Xp Guide
programme.desc: During Elisha’s visit to Oslo, she finds herself in awe of the extraordinary well-designed ships used by the legendary Vikings of Norway.

programme.title: Boarding Pass
programme.desc: Emeline Nsingi Nkosi goes to Santa Village at Rovaniemi, where she reads some of the letters that children from around the world write to him.

programme.title: Xplore Bulgaria
programme.desc: Luxurious hotels, thrilling water parks, inexpensive food and almost 300 days of bright sunny weather make Bulgaria’s biggest seaside resort, Sunny Beach popular for budget beach getaways. Catriona, however, stumbles onto the historic town of Nessebar, which is over 3000 years old, and realizes that there is more to the place than meets the eye. She goes on to visit the Castra Rubra winery, where she finds out about the history of Viticulture in Bulgaria and the process of wine making.

programme.title: City Breaks
programme.desc: Benafsha embarks on a whirlwind adventure in Ahmedabad. With just 48 hours in the city, she is determined to make the most of it, but how?

programme.title: Food Fact Fun
programme.desc: From exploring a vintage car museum to scaling a 5 meter ice-wall and sampling a rich bread cookie, Watch Alex exploring The Hague, also known as the “International city of peace”.

programme.title: 10 Days Scotland
programme.desc: A modern European oil city built on the back of a rich maritime heritage, Aberdeen is the energetic kingpin of northeast Scotland. While the boom times may come and go, the dynamic coastal metropolis never ceases to thrill with its free museums, outdoor adventures and natural attractions right on the city’s doorstep.

programme.title: Backpack Serbia
programme.desc: Heather explores Požarevac and visits the ancient town of Viminacium. Later visits National Museum, concluding with Majdanpek’s Rajkova Pecina Caves.

programme.title: Boarding Pass
programme.desc: Emeline Nsingi Nkosi goes to Rovaniemi, where she visits a husky farm and finds out about about breeding and raising huskies.

programme.title: Xplore Bulgaria
programme.desc: For the people of Sofia, preserving its heritage is sacrosanct, and this can be seen in the way the city's infrastructure is built around its archeological treasures. Catriona realizes this when she roams around the cobblestone streets of the city. With every monument she visits, she finds herself transported to a different era, right from Perperikon fort, which narrate stories of the Byzantine empire, to Boyana church, which is home to some of the earliest examples of medieval artwork.

programme.title: Great World Hotels
programme.desc: From lazing in hammocks by the beach to sipping drinks on a private yacht, Kim realizes that Maldives is the perfect destination to unwind in style.

programme.title: Food Fact Fun
programme.desc: From trying dishes like Stroopwafel and Palat Stoofvlees to visiting the Erasmus bridge, cube houses and wall painting on the streets, Alex explores Rotterdam.

programme.title: 10 Days Scotland
programme.desc: Since medieval times Aberdeenshire and its northwestern neighbour Moray have been the richest and most fertile regions of the Highlands. Aberdeenshire is famed for its Aberdeen Angus beef cattle, its many fine castles and the prosperous 'granite city' of Aberdeen.

programme.title: Backpack Serbia
programme.desc: Explore Majdanpek with our host as the city brings the viewers closer to the treasures strewn across the Serbian countryside.

programme.title: Boarding Pass
programme.desc: Levi is Finland's largest and fastest-growing ski destination. Emeline is here, to find out the exciting activities that Levi offers, in summer.

programme.title: Xplore Bhutan
programme.desc: With snow-capped peaks rising over primeval forests and traditional villages, Paro is like an Oriental Switzerland. On a visit to Paro, Radhika explores famous monasteries such as the Rinpung Dzong and Takstang Monastery, and learns the history and legends associated with them. Along the way, she finds out the secrets to the quintessential Bhutanese peace and contentment.

programme.title: Great World Hotels
programme.desc: An invigorating spa treatment, a cooking lesson, a snorkeling session – Kim has experiences in the Maldives that leave her completely rejuvenated.

programme.title: Food Fact Fun
programme.desc: From trying Orange zest beer and traditional Dutch dishes to exploring St.Martin's Cathedral, Dom Tower and enjoying go karting and stand up paddle boat ride, watch Alex enjoy his trip in Utrecht.

programme.title: 10 Days Scotland
programme.desc: In the enchanting landscapes of Inverness, Robbie’s journey takes a whimsical turn: from eccentric to downright magical.

programme.title: Backpack Serbia
programme.desc: Heather explores Nis,one of the oldest cities in Europe.She visits a concentration camp and later paraglides to end by watching a musical performance.

programme.title: Boarding Pass
programme.desc: Emeline goes to Ruka-Kuusamo, where she tries river rafting. She also meets a reindeer herder,who tells her about the unique ways of herding reindeer.

programme.title: Xplore Bhutan
programme.desc: Actress and travel enthusiast, Radhika Bangia explores the enigma that is Bhutan, when she visits Thimpu, the capital city with no traffic lights and no skyscrapers invading the skyline. Here, she checks out significant sights such as the Tibetan-style National Memorial Chorten, the National Library and the Folk Heritage Museum in order to understand more about the beliefs, history, architecture and cuisine of Bhutan.

programme.title: World Heritage
programme.desc: Explore the ancient heritage sites of Abu Simbel, the Philae complex of Nubia and the Hatshepsut Temple to unravel the mystical stories behind the ancient temples of Egypt.

programme.title: Food Fact Fun
programme.desc: Alex tries traditional Dutch food in Leiden, learns about Dutch history at Prinsenhof Museum, visits a Gothic church, a local cheese market and Delftware factory at Gouda.

programme.title: 10 Days Scotland
programme.desc: The grand finale of Robbie’s wild homecoming unfolds amidst the rugged beauty of the Isle of Skye.

programme.title: Backpack Serbia
programme.desc: Continue exploring Serbia with Heather as she visits the Manasija monastery, Resavska caves, ending with a joyride onboard Eight of Sargan rail line.

programme.title: With Love From Mauritius
programme.desc: Krisann and Nathan’s vacation in Mauritius begins in Le Morne, where an unexpected adventure awaits the latter.

programme.title: Xplore Bhutan
programme.desc: Known as Druk Yul or the “Land of the Thunder Dragon”, Bhutan is the country of mountains, monasteries and mystery. Radhika Bangia finds this to be true when she visits Punakha, the former capital of Bhutan, where she marvels at the architecture of Punakha Dzong and gets to know the story behind the two rivers, Mo Chhu and Po Chhu. She also explores the sacred temples and Mebar Tsho lake of Bumthang, the religious heart of Bhutan, and gets to know the local legends.

programme.title: World Spas
programme.desc: Jamie Srubis tries out spa treatments, which are inspired by the Dead Sea and the desert surroundings at Hotel Kempinski Ishtar in Jordan.

programme.title: Food Fact Fun
programme.desc: After Burj Khalifa, Alex has a traditional Emirati platter, visits the gold market and skiing rink, and goes for a camel safari and camping.

programme.title: City break
programme.desc: Nestled in the mighty Alps, tour the Austrian city of Innsbruck with Sabrina and explore a town that’s alpine-urban yet traditional in nature.

programme.title: Backpack Serbia
programme.desc: In her final leg of journey, Heather visits the Uvac canyon, tries locally prepared pie, ending with Kopaonik National Park and Jelovarnik waterfalls.

programme.title: With Love From Mauritius
programme.desc: On an island-hopping adventure in Mauritius, Krisann and Nathan find out that time works differently on these islands.

programme.title: XP Guide
programme.desc: A homely place where the aroma of freshly baked bread greets you as people smoothly glide along on their bicycles. Welcome to Copenhagen.

programme.title: Unwind
programme.desc: Indeya explores the Cinnamon plantations, tries a local dish and witnesses a traditional dance performance.

programme.title: Food Fact Fun
programme.desc: Alex has varied experiences in Dubai like trying Lebanese cheese bread, visiting a coffee museum, going to IMG Worlds of Adventure and more.

programme.title: Food Fact Fun
programme.desc: In Amsterdam, Alex tries Bitterballen, cannabis cake and visits Heineken Experience brewery. He explores Zaanse Schans village with windmills and Coster Diamonds, the oldest diamond polishing factory.

programme.title: 10 Days Scotland
programme.desc: Robbie’s journey to reconnect with his Scottish roots begins in Edinburgh, the stunning capital city perched on extinct volcanoes. Navigating the twists of the Royal Mile, strutting around the majestic Edinburgh castle and learning to sip scotch like a true Scotsman, he feels a tad closer to his heritage. The real fun kicks in, however, when he stumbles onto a rather flamboyant way to unleash his inner Highlander.

programme.title: Xplore Bulgaria
programme.desc: Like Rome, Plovdiv straddles seven hills; but as Europe’s oldest continuously inhabited city, it’s far more ancient. As Catriona takes a tour of the city to see its iconic attractions such as a Roman Amphitheatre, which was once the setting for gladiator fights, and the striking Baroque Saints Constantine and Helena Church, she begins to see how seamlessly modern life blends with the age-old ruins. Along the way, she realizes why Plovdiv is called the ‘Eternal City’ of Bulgaria.

programme.title: XP Guide
programme.desc: Stockholm, the capital and largest city of Sweden, was built on 14 Islands. This place is also sometimes referred as Venice of the north.

programme.desc: This exhilarating city of castles, beer and segway rides can take you on a break that will truly leave you catching your breath.

programme.title: Brindiamo! With Ornella Fado
programme.desc: Ornella Fado continua il suo giro per incontrare il suoi amici ristoratori durante le festività di capodanno. Visitando a midtown Chef Pino alla "La Masseria", chef Stefano e Franco a " Viceversa", a "PizzArte" incontra il pizzaiolo Roberto ed il proprietario Dario, a Queens visita "Ornella Trattoria" dove incontra Vincenzo e "Trattoria l'Incontro"dove incontra lo chef e proprietario Rocco. (2013/2014)

programme.title: Eccellenze Italiane con Piero Muscari intervista a Heinz Beck
programme.desc: Piero Muscari intervista Patrizio Podini, presidente MD S.p.A. Condividendo storie della sua infanzia, Patrizia racconta le origini del suo lavoro nel commercio della distribuzione e come si è evoluto nel tempo.

programme.title: Eccellenze Italiane con Piero Muscari intervista a Heinz Beck
programme.desc: Piero Muscari intervista Franco Ricci, Presidente Fondazione Italiana Sommelier. Franco racconta l'importanza della cultura del vino in Italia e come la sua fondazione ha rivoluzionato questo settore in oltre 20 anni.

programme.title: My Talented Friends Season 1
programme.desc: Ornella Fado presenta il lavoro artistico della sua amica Geniale,Paola Sinisgalli fondatrice della "Creative Shakes" il lavoro artistico di oggi e` un corto che vede Paola nei panni di regista.

programme.title: My Talented Friends Season 1
programme.desc: Ornella Fado introuce il suo amico Geniale Marco Napoli, attore e regista televisivo e cinematografico. Il documentario di oggi vede come protagonista una giovane artista catanese Roberta Finocchiaro "Save lives with the Rhytm.

programme.title: Il CIRCOLO DEL BUONGUSTAI Con Fabio Campoli
programme.desc: Benvenuti a questo nuovo episodio del Circolo dei Buongustai! Cosa bolle in pentola? Oggi il nostro chef Fabio Campoli ci delizierà con tre ricette speciali: rotolo di riso, prosciutto e melone, risotto alla rucola con kiwi e vongole, e budino di riso. Buona visione a tutti!

programme.title: Il CIRCOLO DEL BUONGUSTAI Con Fabio Campoli
programme.desc: Benvenuti a questo nuovo episodio del Circolo dei Buongustai! Cosa bolle in pentola? Oggi il nostro chef Fabio Campoli ci delizierà con tre ricette speciali: cono di stesa, venere nera, e babà al bergamotto. Buona visione a tutti!

programme.title: Change your Destiny
programme.desc: Nella prima puntata Antonio Fini ed i suoi giudici incontrano il primo gruppo di ballerini.Per essere selezionati per il prestigioso festival di danza in NEW YORK devono affrontare 3 sfide: presentare il loro pezzo migliore, la contemporary battle e la lezione di danza "tecnica Graham" con il maestro Antonio Fini.

programme.title: Change your Destiny
programme.desc: Continuano le audizioni di Antonio Fini e dei suoi giudici per selezionare i migliori danzatori da invitare al prestigioso festival di danza in NEW YORK. I ragazzi affrontano le tre sfide previste: portare il loro pezzo migliore, la contemporary battle e la lezione di Hip hop con Mirco Giordano e Marcello Gargarelli.

programme.title: Change your Destiny
programme.desc: Terza ed ultima puntata della serie. Antonio Fini ed i suoi giudici sono pronti: chi sceglieranno per volare a NEW YORK per il prestigioso festival della danza? Ancora una volta i ragazzi devono convincere l'importante giuria con il loro pezzo migliore, la contemporary battle e la lezione di danza "tecnica Parson" con la maestra Abby Silva.

programme.title: Brindiamo! With Ornella Fado
programme.desc: Ornella Fado visita cinque amici ristoratori durante le feste Natalizie, prima tappa" Angelina's" a Staten Island, poi e` la volta di "Marco Polo" e "Luzzo" a Brooklyn. A Manhattan nell'Upper East side, Ornella visita "83 e mezza". Ultima tappa per ujn buon gelato,"Dolce Gelateria" nel Village. (2013)

programme.title: Brindiamo! With Ornella Fado
programme.desc: Ornella Fado si trova nel laboratorio di Urbani Truffle a New York ed invita i suoi amici chef per preparare insieme delle ricette da gustare durante le festività natalizie. In questa puntata Chef Pino Coladonato, Chef Marcello Russodivito e la giovane pizzaiola Giorgia Caporuscio. In questa edizione di Brindiamo! in my Kitchen ospite speciale la soprano Cristina Fontanelli e John La Barbera, oltre a vari ospiti del mondo dello spettacolo, e della moda. (2016)

programme.title: Brindiamo! With Ornella Fado
programme.desc: Ornella Fado si trova nel laboratorio di Urbani Truffle a New York per la serconda parte di Brindiamo! in my kitchen. I suoi amici chef in questa puntata son lo chef Raffaele Ronca, lo Chef Marco Chirico e il giovane pizzaiolo Ettore Toches. In questa edizione di Brindiamo! in my Kitchen la musica è dal vivo con la soprano Cristina Fontanelli ed il compositore Jhon La Barbera, tra gli ospiti di questa puntata il Fashion designer Domenico Vacca. (2016)

programme.title: Brindiamo! With Ornella Fado
programme.desc: Ornella Fado visita il ristorante "Lincoln Square Steak House", a due passi da Lincoln Center. In questa puntata Ornella prepara alcune ricette con il Principe Emanule Filiberto di Savoia, gia` ospite dell'evento di Carnevale di alcuni giorni prima, lo chef Marcello Russodivito insegna al Principe come preprare una succulenta bistecca e prepara un piatto dedicato alla Regina Margherita. Questa puntata fa parte della stagione speciale "Brindiamo! International Cuisine". (2016)

programme.title: Brindiamo! With Ornella Fado
programme.desc: Ornella Fado visita il ristorante di "Angelina's" a Staten Island per preparare alcune ricette con Vincent Malerba proprietario del ristorante, ospite di eccezione la cantante Giada Valenti che oltre a preparare una ricetta con Vincent racconta ad Ornella il suo sogno americano. (2016)

programme.title: Brindiamo! With Ornella Fado
programme.desc: Ornella Fado con lo chef Raffaele Ronca, visitano il famoso ristorante giapponese "Sushi Nakasawa", oltre a preprare sushi con Chef Nakasawa, lo chef Raffaele Ronca prepara un piatto a quattro mani con Daisuke Nakasawa e poi un sei mani con Alessandro Borgognone, co- proprietario del ristorante, questa puntata fa parte della stagione speciale "Brindiamo! International Cuisine". (2016)

programme.title: Brindiamo! With Ornella Fado
programme.desc: In questo epsiodio di Brindiamo! Ornella Fado fa un viaggio dei suoi momenti televisivi. Intervista il famoso chef Joy Campanaro e con chef Ronca preparano gli spaghetti alla chitarra, fa visita a Vincenzo Cutrone allo stabilmento di Brooklyn, "Octopus Garden", presenta una competizione per la regione Basilicata al ristorante Marco Polo, e visita lo chef Marcello Russodivito che festeggia 30 anni del ristorante e conduce un concerto a Suffern NY. (2016)

programme.title: Brindiamo!
programme.desc: Questa puntata fa parte della serie vintage. Ornella Fado visita due ristoranti di eccellenza, San Pietro e Masseria. Al San Pietro Ornella incontra i fratelli Bruno, con lo chef Antonio prepara delle ricette gustose del classico menù del ristorante, mentre Gerardo racconta la storia del rstorante ed il successo dei loro ristoranti. Da Masseria Ornella incontra lo Chef e Patron Pino Coladonato, e preparano delle ricette tipiche pugliesi.

programme.title: Brindiamo!
programme.desc: Questa puntata fa parte della serie vintage. Ornella Fado visita" Via Oreto", un ristorante dal cuore siciliano. Antony prepara le ricette della nonna e della mamma. Accanto la nonna a visionare tutto. In questo episodio Ornella intervsita un esperto del vino siciliano Pietro Cavallo, e parlano dell'importanza di abbinare il giusto vino ad ogni piatto.

programme.title: Brindiamo!
programme.desc: Questa puntata fa parte della serie vintage. Ornella Fado visita Gente, dove incontra Jay Mitchel un americano appassionato di Italia al punto di aprire un suo ristorante in america per far apprezzare il cibo Italiano ad i suoi clienti.

programme.title: Brindiamo!
programme.desc: Questa puntata fa parte della serie vintage. Ornella Fado visita il ristorante Barolo, nel cuore di Soho ed incontra il proprietario Paolo Secondo, che oltre a preparare una squsita focaccia Genovese, racconta la sua storia di giovane imprenditore italiano a New York.

programme.title: Brindiamo!
programme.desc: In questa puntata, Ornella Fado conduce un favoloso evento dedicato alla Regione Calabria da Eataly Downtown, preparando ricette insieme allo chef calabrese Luca Caligiuro e allo chef italo-americano Ryan Rizzuto.

programme.title: Brindiamo!
programme.desc: Ornella Fado è a Milano, all'EXPO, con i giovani della Basilicata del progetto Sano è Lucano. I prodotti tipici lucani ispirano una serie di incontri culinari: dalla barca alla Mondadori, fino a Lady Bu Bistrò e al ristorante stellato Il Luogo di Aimo e Nadia con lo chef Fabio Pisani.Il racconto è arricchito dagli interventi di Sara Farnetti e Pietro Bitonti.

programme.title: Brindiamo!
programme.desc: In questa puntata, Ornella Fado conduce un favoloso evento dedicato alla Regione Calabria da Eataly Downtown, preparando ricette insieme allo chef calabrese Luca Caligiuro e allo chef italo-americano Ryan Rizzuto.

programme.title: Eccellenze Italiane con Piero Muscari intervista a Heinz Beck
programme.desc: Piero Muscari intervista Franco Ricci, Presidente Fondazione Italiana Sommelier. Franco racconta l'importanza della cultura del vino in Italia e come la sua fondazione ha rivoluzionato questo settore in oltre 20 anni.

programme.title: Brindiamo! With Ornella Fado
programme.desc: Ornella Fado visita cinque amici ristoratori durante le feste Natalizie, prima tappa" Angelina's" a Staten Island, poi e` la volta di "Marco Polo" e "Luzzo" a Brooklyn. A Manhattan nell'Upper East side, Ornella visita "83 e mezza". Ultima tappa per ujn buon gelato,"Dolce Gelateria" nel Village. (2013)

programme.title: Il CIRCOLO DEL BUONGUSTAI Con Fabio Campoli
programme.desc: Benvenuti a questo nuovo episodio del Circolo dei Buongustai! Cosa bolle in pentola? Oggi il nostro chef Fabio Campoli ci delizierà con tre ricette speciali: cono di stesa, venere nera, e babà al bergamotto. Buona visione a tutti!

programme.title: Il CIRCOLO DEL BUONGUSTAI Con Fabio Campoli
programme.desc: Benvenuti a questo nuovo episodio del Circolo dei Buongustai! Cosa bolle in pentola? Oggi il nostro chef Fabio Campoli ci delizierà con tre ricette speciali: portafoglio di squacquerone, ciliegie e mortadella, riso rosso integrale con pollo e peperoni, diplomatico alle rose. Buona visione a tutti!

programme.title: Il CIRCOLO DEL BUONGUSTAI Con Fabio Campoli
programme.desc: Benvenuti a questo nuovo episodio del Circolo dei Buongustai! Cosa bolle in pentola? Oggi il nostro chef Fabio Campoli ci delizierà con tre ricette speciali: rotolo di riso, prosciutto e melone, risotto alla rucola con kiwi e vongole, e budino di riso. Buona visione a tutti!

programme.title: Change your Destiny
programme.desc: Terza ed ultima puntata della serie. Antonio Fini ed i suoi giudici sono pronti: chi sceglieranno per volare a NEW YORK per il prestigioso festival della danza? Ancora una volta i ragazzi devono convincere l'importante giuria con il loro pezzo migliore, la contemporary battle e la lezione di danza "tecnica Parson" con la maestra Abby Silva.

programme.title: Change your Destiny
programme.desc: Nella prima puntata Antonio Fini ed i suoi giudici incontrano il primo gruppo di ballerini.Per essere selezionati per il prestigioso festival di danza in NEW YORK devono affrontare 3 sfide: presentare il loro pezzo migliore, la contemporary battle e la lezione di danza "tecnica Graham" con il maestro Antonio Fini.

programme.title: Brindiamo!
programme.desc: La puntata di oggi è ricca di contenuti. Ornella Fado incontra Cristina Carrani, esperta in materia immobiliare che aiuterà Ornella a scoprire l'Isola di Capri. Dalla moda alla storia, al cibo e all'arte, Brindiamo! Edizione speciale Capri è l'episodio perfetto per festeggiare i 18 anni di Brindiamo! su NYC LIFE.

programme.title: Brindiamo! With Ornella Fado
programme.desc: Ornella Fado si trova nel laboratorio di Urbani Truffle a New York ed invita i suoi amici chef per preparare insieme delle ricette da gustare durante le festività natalizie. In questa puntata Chef Pino Coladonato, Chef Marcello Russodivito e la giovane pizzaiola Giorgia Caporuscio. In questa edizione di Brindiamo! in my Kitchen ospite speciale la soprano Cristina Fontanelli e John La Barbera, oltre a vari ospiti del mondo dello spettacolo, e della moda. (2016)

programme.title: Brindiamo! With Ornella Fado
programme.desc: Ornella Fado si trova nel laboratorio di Urbani Truffle a New York per la serconda parte di Brindiamo! in my kitchen. I suoi amici chef in questa puntata son lo chef Raffaele Ronca, lo Chef Marco Chirico e il giovane pizzaiolo Ettore Toches. In questa edizione di Brindiamo! in my Kitchen la musica è dal vivo con la soprano Cristina Fontanelli ed il compositore Jhon La Barbera, tra gli ospiti di questa puntata il Fashion designer Domenico Vacca. (2016)

programme.title: Brindiamo! With Ornella Fado
programme.desc: Ornella Fado visita cinque amici ristoratori durante le feste Natalizie, prima tappa" Angelina's" a Staten Island, poi e` la volta di "Marco Polo" e "Luzzo" a Brooklyn. A Manhattan nell'Upper East side, Ornella visita "83 e mezza". Ultima tappa per ujn buon gelato,"Dolce Gelateria" nel Village. (2013)

programme.title: Brindiamo! With Ornella Fado
programme.desc: Ornella Fado continua il suo giro per incontrare il suoi amici ristoratori durante le festività di capodanno. Visitando a midtown Chef Pino alla "La Masseria", chef Stefano e Franco a " Viceversa", a "PizzArte" incontra il pizzaiolo Roberto ed il proprietario Dario, a Queens visita "Ornella Trattoria" dove incontra Vincenzo e "Trattoria l'Incontro"dove incontra lo chef e proprietario Rocco. (2013/2014)

programme.title: Brindiamo! With Ornella Fado
programme.desc: Ornella Fado visita Joe e Marco Chirico, al nuovo Marco Polo ristorante, il ristorante è stato appena ristrutturato ed ha un look fresco e moderno. Oltre a preparare le ricette del nuovo menù, Ornella prepara una ricetta con un ospite speciale, Monsignor Jamie Gigantiello, Monsignore Gigantiello prepara la ricetta della nonna, polpette di melenzane. Nel ristorante accanto Chef Marco prepara sfizi per i fan di Brindiamo! tra cui una pizza degli spiedini e un super cocktail. (2014)

programme.title: Brindiamo! With Ornella Fado
programme.desc: Ornella Fado visita uno dei nuovi trendy ristoranti nel Village in New York City." Rafele" ristorante. Ornella e Chef Raffaele Ronca, prima di iniziare il loro viaggio culinario, fanno shopping al "Green market"a Union Square. Oltre a preparare ricette tipiche Napoletane,lo chef preprara alcune ricette con ingredienti Calabresi e per concludere in dolcezza e Bellezza prepara una sua super specialità la famosa torta di ricotta una delle "Cheese cake' più famose di New York. (2014)

programme.title: Brindiamo! With Ornella Fado
programme.desc: Ornella Fado si trova nel laboratorio di Urbani Truffle a New York ed invita i suoi amici chef per preparare insieme delle ricette da gustare durante le festività natalizie. In questa puntata Chef Pino Coladonato, Chef Marcello Russodivito e la giovane pizzaiola Giorgia Caporuscio. In questa edizione di Brindiamo! in my Kitchen ospite speciale la soprano Cristina Fontanelli e John La Barbera, oltre a vari ospiti del mondo dello spettacolo, e della moda. (2016)

programme.title: Brindiamo! With Ornella Fado
programme.desc: Ornella Fado si trova nel laboratorio di Urbani Truffle a New York per la serconda parte di Brindiamo! in my kitchen. I suoi amici chef in questa puntata son lo chef Raffaele Ronca, lo Chef Marco Chirico e il giovane pizzaiolo Ettore Toches. In questa edizione di Brindiamo! in my Kitchen la musica è dal vivo con la soprano Cristina Fontanelli ed il compositore Jhon La Barbera, tra gli ospiti di questa puntata il Fashion designer Domenico Vacca. (2016)

programme.title: Brindiamo! With Ornella Fado
programme.desc: Ornella Fado visita il ristorante "Lincoln Square Steak House", a due passi da Lincoln Center. In questa puntata Ornella prepara alcune ricette con il Principe Emanule Filiberto di Savoia, gia` ospite dell'evento di Carnevale di alcuni giorni prima, lo chef Marcello Russodivito insegna al Principe come preprare una succulenta bistecca e prepara un piatto dedicato alla Regina Margherita. Questa puntata fa parte della stagione speciale "Brindiamo! International Cuisine". (2016)

programme.title: Brindiamo! With Ornella Fado
programme.desc: Ornella Fado visita il ristorante di "Angelina's" a Staten Island per preparare alcune ricette con Vincent Malerba proprietario del ristorante, ospite di eccezione la cantante Giada Valenti che oltre a preparare una ricetta con Vincent racconta ad Ornella il suo sogno americano. (2016)

programme.title: Brindiamo! With Ornella Fado
programme.desc: Ornella Fado con lo chef Raffaele Ronca, visitano il famoso ristorante giapponese "Sushi Nakasawa", oltre a preprare sushi con Chef Nakasawa, lo chef Raffaele Ronca prepara un piatto a quattro mani con Daisuke Nakasawa e poi un sei mani con Alessandro Borgognone, co- proprietario del ristorante, questa puntata fa parte della stagione speciale "Brindiamo! International Cuisine". (2016)

programme.title: Brindiamo! With Ornella Fado
programme.desc: In questo epsiodio di Brindiamo! Ornella Fado fa un viaggio dei suoi momenti televisivi. Intervista il famoso chef Joy Campanaro e con chef Ronca preparano gli spaghetti alla chitarra, fa visita a Vincenzo Cutrone allo stabilmento di Brooklyn, "Octopus Garden", presenta una competizione per la regione Basilicata al ristorante Marco Polo, e visita lo chef Marcello Russodivito che festeggia 30 anni del ristorante e conduce un concerto a Suffern NY. (2016)

programme.title: Brindiamo! With Ornella Fado

A Manhattan nell'Upper East side, Ornella visita "83 e mezza". Ultima tappa per ujn buon gelato,"Dolce Gelateria" nel Village. (2013)

programme.title: Change your Destiny
programme.desc: Continuano le audizioni di Antonio Fini e dei suoi giudici per selezionare i migliori danzatori da invitare al prestigioso festival di danza in NEW YORK. programme.title: My Talented Friends Season 1
programme.desc: Ornella Fado introuce il suo amico Geniale Marco Napoli, attore e regista televisivo e cinematografico. Il documentario di oggi vede come protagonista una giovane artista catanese Roberta Finocchiaro "Save lives with the Rhytm.

programme.title: My Talented Friends Season 1
programme.desc: Ornella Fado introuce il suo amico Geniale Marco Napoli, attore e regista televisivo e cinematografico. Il documentario di oggi vede come protagonista una giovane artista catanese Roberta Finocchiaro "Save lives with the Rhytm.

programme.title: Eccellenze Italiane con Piero Muscari intervista a Heinz Beck
programme.desc: Piero Muscari intervista Patrizio Podini, presidente MD S.p.A. Condividendo storie della sua infanzia, Patrizia racconta le origini del suo lavoro nel commercio della distribuzione e come si è evoluto nel tempo.

programme.title: Eccellenze Italiane con Piero Muscari intervista a Heinz Beck
programme.desc: Piero Muscari intervista Franco Ricci, Presidente Fondazione Italiana Sommelier. Franco racconta l'importanza della cultura del vino in Italia e come la sua fondazione ha rivoluzionato questo settore in oltre 20 anni.

programme.title: Brindiamo!
programme.desc: Questa puntata fa parte della serie vintage. Ornella Fado visita due ristoranti di eccellenza, San Pietro e Masseria. Al San Pietro Ornella incontra i fratelli Bruno, con lo chef Antonio prepara delle ricette gustose del classico menù del ristorante, mentre Gerardo racconta la storia del rstorante ed il successo dei loro ristoranti. Da Masseria Ornella incontra lo Chef e Patron Pino Coladonato, e preparano delle ricette tipiche pugliesi.

programme.title: Brindiamo!
programme.desc: Questa puntata fa parte della serie vintage. Ornella Fado visita" Via Oreto", un ristorante dal cuore siciliano. Antony prepara le ricette della nonna e della mamma. Accanto la nonna a visionare tutto. In questo episodio Ornella intervsita un esperto del vino siciliano Pietro Cavallo, e parlano dell'importanza di abbinare il giusto vino ad ogni piatto.

programme.title: Brindiamo!
programme.desc: Questa puntata fa parte della serie vintage. Ornella Fado visita Gente, dove incontra Jay Mitchel un americano appassionato di Italia al punto di aprire un suo ristorante in america per far apprezzare il cibo Italiano ad i suoi clienti.

programme.title: Brindiamo!
programme.desc: Questa puntata fa parte della serie vintage. Ornella Fado visita il ristorante Barolo, nel cuore di Soho ed incontra il proprietario Paolo Secondo, che oltre a preparare una squsita focaccia Genovese, racconta la sua storia di giovane imprenditore italiano a New York.

programme.title: My Talented Friends Season 1
programme.desc: Ornella Fado presenta il lavoro artistico della sua amica Geniale,Paola Sinisgalli fondatrice della "Creative Shakes" il lavoro artistico di oggi e` un corto che vede Paola nei panni di regista.

programme.title: My Talented Friends Season 1
programme.desc: Ornella Fado introuce il suo amico Geniale Marco Napoli, attore e regista televisivo e cinematografico. Il documentario di oggi vede come protagonista una giovane artista catanese Roberta Finocchiaro "Save lives with the Rhytm.
